title: poa aucklandica subsp. rakiura
intro: Poa aucklandica subsp. rakiura, also known as Rakiura tussock, is a rare and endemic subspecies of grass native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional Maori medicine, the Rakiura tussock is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to utilize its medicinal properties.
